sections: Stock, Archive & Document Storage for Sussex Businesses

Running a business means juggling space you do not always have. Whether you are holding seasonal stock, freeing up a back office, or finally clearing out years of paper records that legally have to be kept, our secure business storage in Sussex gives you somewhere clean, dry and properly accountable to put it. As a family-run firm based at Doryln House on London Road in Ashington, near Pulborough RH20 3JT, we have been helping companies across West Sussex and the wider South East store their goods since 2016, and we treat your inventory with the same care we would our own.

Our containerised units suit a wide range of commercial needs, and because everything is itemised when it enters store, you always know exactly what you have and where it sits. Typical uses we handle every week include:

  • Trading stock and surplus inventory that needs to be kept out of valuable retail or warehouse space
  • Business archives and document storage, including records you must retain for compliance and tax purposes
  • Office furniture, desks, chairs and partitioning during a refit, relocation or downsize
  • IT equipment, servers and hardware held safely between premises moves
  • Marketing materials, exhibition stands, point-of-sale displays and promotional stock
Office Furniture & IT Equipment Between Premises

Few things disrupt a business like a half-finished move. When you are changing premises, refurbishing a floor, or consolidating two sites into one, there is almost always a period where furniture and equipment have nowhere sensible to live. Rather than crowding a working office with stacked desks or leaving expensive kit exposed on a busy site, we collect, wrap and transport it into store, then return it the moment your new space is ready. It is a natural extension of our commercial removals service, and clients often book the two together so a single team handles the whole project end to end.

IT equipment in particular deserves careful handling. Servers, monitors, printers and networking hardware are sensitive to damp, dust and rough movement, which is exactly why our clean, dry, containerised units are well suited to holding them. Each item is padded, labelled and logged so nothing is mislaid, and our crews are trained to load technology so it travels without damage. We are fully insured with £10m liability cover, and every commercial contract is handled with the same professionalism that has earned us Checkatrade verification and recommendations from agents including Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart.

For retailers and online sellers, storage is rarely a fixed need — it rises and falls with the seasons. The run-up to Christmas, summer ranges, sale stock and supplier bulk-buys all create peaks where you simply need more space than your unit or shop can offer. Our overflow storage gives you that breathing room without the cost and commitment of a permanent warehouse lease. Take a single container when demand spikes, scale back when it eases, and never pay for square footage you are not using.

Because we also run a contract delivery service, e-commerce businesses can pair storage with regular collections and drops, so stock moves smoothly from our units out to customers or other locations. If your needs are short and sharp around a busy trading window, our short-term storage is the natural fit; if you want a settled base for slower-moving lines, long-term storage works out more economical the longer you stay. We will talk through your trading pattern and recommend the arrangement that genuinely saves you money rather than the one that fills the most space.

When you hand over business stock, archives or equipment, you need to know it is genuinely safe and properly accounted for — not just dumped in a shed. Our storage is secure, alarmed and weatherproof, and the containerised approach means your goods sit in their own dedicated unit rather than loose among other people’s belongings. Every item is inventoried as it goes into store, so you have a clear written record of exactly what you have placed with us. That paper trail matters for insurance, for audits, and simply for your own peace of mind.

Access is arranged by appointment so we can prepare your unit and have your goods ready, which keeps the site orderly and your stock secure. If you need an item retrieved, a part-delivery made, or fresh stock added, our team handles the lifting and logging for you. The practical benefits our commercial clients value most include:

  • Dedicated containerised units — your goods are never mixed with another customer’s
  • Clean, dry and weatherproof conditions that protect stock, paper and electronics alike
  • A full written inventory taken on intake, giving you an auditable record
  • Alarmed, monitored premises with managed, appointment-based access
  • Hands-on help with loading, retrieval and rotation so you are not lifting heavy items yourself
How Business Storage Works With Us

We keep the process simple because business owners do not have time for complicated arrangements. It begins with a conversation about what you need to store, for how long, and how often you expect to need access. From there we recommend the right number of units and the most cost-effective term, then arrange collection from your premises — or you can deliver to us at Ashington if you prefer. Our crew wraps, protects and itemises everything as it is loaded, so the inventory is built as the work happens.

Once your goods are in store, they stay in their sealed containerised unit until you ask for them back, in whole or in part. There are no long, locked-in commitments designed to trap you; terms are flexible because real business needs flex. When you are ready, get a clear, no-obligation figure through our online quote form, take a quick look at our transparent storage pricing, or use the storage calculator to estimate how much space your stock and equipment will actually need before you commit to a single thing.

Over the years we have stored for a broad mix of South East businesses, and the common thread is always the same — they need flexible, trustworthy space without the overhead of running their own facility. Independent retailers use us for seasonal overflow; online sellers lean on us for stock they cannot fit at home or in a small unit; offices and professional firms store furniture during refits and archives long term; and tradespeople park tools, materials and equipment between jobs. We cover West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, with UK and European reach when a project needs it.

Being a family-run, LAPADA-member firm means accountability is personal here. You deal with the people who actually handle your goods, not a faceless call centre, and the same care that has earned recommendations from respected estate agents across the region goes into every commercial job. To understand how we work and the standards we hold ourselves to, read more about our family-run Sussex team — or call us on 01903 893731 to talk through exactly what your business needs to store.
faqs: Can I store business documents and archive records with you?

Yes. Our clean, dry, containerised units are well suited to archive and document storage, including records you are legally required to retain for tax or compliance purposes. Everything is inventoried on intake so you have a clear written record, and access is arranged by appointment whenever you need to retrieve a file or box.
Do I have to commit to a long contract for commercial storage?

No. Our terms are flexible because business needs change. If you only need space around a busy trading season, our short-term storage is ideal; if you want a settled long-term base for slower-moving stock, our long-term storage becomes more economical the longer you stay. We will recommend the arrangement that actually saves you money.
How do I know how much storage space my stock needs?

The easiest way is our storage calculator, which helps you estimate the number of units your stock, furniture or equipment will require. If you would rather talk it through, call us on 01903 893731 or email contact@wolves-removals.co.uk and we will help you size it accurately before you commit.
Can you collect our stock and equipment from our premises?

Yes. We collect from business premises across West Sussex, East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, wrapping, protecting and itemising your goods as we load. Many clients combine storage with our commercial removals or contract delivery service so a single team manages the whole job from collection to return.
